_doc: The baseline pollenwatch version the cleanroom test installs FIRST, before upgrading to HEAD. Default is v3.0.0 — the first release of the RESET schema (config-entry VERSION=1, unified 'selected_species' key, async_migrate_entry removed). The no-loss gate only holds WITHIN the same schema generation, so the baseline must stay >= v3.0.0. Override with --baseline <tag> for smoke runs or future baselines.

v3.0.0.notes: Schema RESET release. NOTE: 'flow_version' here is the config-FLOW SHAPE the cleanroom harness drives (ha_flow.py branches <=2 = single-step 'allergens', >=3 = TWO-step user→species with 'selected_species') — NOT the config-entry storage VERSION. v3.0.0 reset the storage VERSION to 1 (removed the v1→v2→v3 migration chain + the legacy 'allergens' alias) but KEPT the two-step selected_species flow shape, so flow_version stays 3. First release of the current schema generation — the correct cleanroom baseline going forward.

v2.1.0.notes: PRE-RESET (historical). VERSION=3, TWO-step flow. Will NOT upgrade cleanly to v3.0.0+ — the v3.0.0 breaking change removed migration, so a v2.x entry (version 3) cannot load on VERSION=1 code. Do not use as a no-loss baseline.

v2.0.0.notes: PRE-RESET (historical). First v3-schema release, VERSION=3. Same caveat as v2.1.0 — not a valid baseline for v3.0.0+.

v1.3.0.notes: PRE-RESET (historical). Last v1.x release, VERSION=2, legacy 'allergens' key. Not a valid baseline for v3.0.0+ (the migration that bridged it was removed in v3.0.0).

_when_to_update: Add a new entry under 'versions' when a new release is tagged AND its flow VERSION or species field changed. Bootstrap errors loudly on unknown baseline tag. Keep 'default_baseline' on the latest release of the CURRENT schema generation (>= v3.0.0); only bump it across another deliberate breaking schema change.
